title: Backup and Disaster Recovery Policy
content:
Backup and Disaster Recovery Policy v2.5 — Aligned with NIST SP 800-34, NIST SP 800-184, and ISO 22301.

1. BACKUP REQUIREMENTS
1.1 All business-critical systems and data must be backed up according to the following schedule:
  a) Tier 1 (Mission Critical) — RPO: 1 hour, RTO: 4 hours. Examples: ERP, CRM, email, authentication services, databases.
  b) Tier 2 (Business Important) — RPO: 4 hours, RTO: 12 hours. Examples: file servers, collaboration tools, development environments.
  c) Tier 3 (Non-Critical) — RPO: 24 hours, RTO: 48 hours. Examples: test environments, archived data, training systems.
1.2 RPO (Recovery Point Objective) defines the maximum acceptable data loss. RTO (Recovery Time Objective) defines the maximum acceptable downtime.

2. BACKUP STRATEGY
2.1 Backups must follow the 3-2-1-1 rule: 3 copies of data, on 2 different media types, 1 offsite copy, 1 immutable/air-gapped copy.
2.2 Full backups must be performed weekly for all tiers.
2.3 Incremental or differential backups must be performed daily (Tier 1 and 2) or weekly (Tier 3).
2.4 Transaction log backups for databases must occur every 15 minutes for Tier 1 systems.
2.5 Backup data must be encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+).
2.6 Immutable backups (write-once, read-many) must be maintained for Tier 1 systems to protect against ransomware.

3. BACKUP VERIFICATION AND TESTING
3.1 Backup integrity verification must be automated and run after every backup job.
3.2 Restore tests must be performed: monthly for Tier 1, quarterly for Tier 2, semi-annually for Tier 3.
3.3 Full disaster recovery simulation must be conducted annually, including failover to the DR site.
3.4 Restore test results must be documented with: time to restore, data integrity verification, and any issues encountered.
3.5 Failed backup jobs must generate alerts to the operations team within 15 minutes and be resolved within 4 hours.

4. DISASTER RECOVERY
4.1 A documented Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP) must exist for every Tier 1 and Tier 2 system.
4.2 DR plans must include: recovery procedures, team contact information, vendor escalation paths, and communication templates.
4.3 The DR site must be geographically separated from the primary site by a minimum of 100 miles.
4.4 DR failover must be tested semi-annually with documented results.
4.5 DR plans must account for the following scenarios: datacenter loss, ransomware, cloud provider outage, network disruption, and key personnel unavailability.

5. RETENTION
5.1 Daily backups: retained for 30 days.
5.2 Weekly backups: retained for 90 days.
5.3 Monthly backups: retained for 1 year.
5.4 Annual backups: retained for 7 years or as required by regulatory obligations.
5.5 Backup retention must comply with the Data Retention Schedule maintained by Legal.

6. CLOUD BACKUP CONSIDERATIONS
6.1 Cloud-hosted systems must utilize cloud-native backup solutions with cross-region replication.
6.2 Cloud backup encryption keys must be customer-managed (BYOK), not provider-managed.
6.3 Cloud backup restoration must be tested quarterly to verify data portability and avoid vendor lock-in.

7. REFERENCES
- NIST SP 800-34 Rev 1 Contingency Planning Guide
- NIST SP 800-184 Guide for Cybersecurity Event Recovery
- ISO 22301:2019 Business Continuity Management
- CIS Controls v8 Control 11
- DoD Instruction 8500.01 Cybersecurity
